name: I slipped at work in Salt Lake City and hurt my back, what do I do right now?
description: Report to employer immediately and get medical care; Utah gives 180 days but sooner avoids issues.[1]

mainEntity.name: I slipped at work in Salt Lake City and hurt my back, what do I do right now?

acceptedAnswer.text: Report to employer immediately and get medical care; Utah gives 180 days but sooner avoids issues.[1]
acceptedAnswer.description: Report the injury to your employer immediately, either verbally or in writing, and seek medical attention from an approved provider. Your employer must file the Employer’s First Report of Injury within 7 days, and your doctor submits a Physician’s Initial Report.[1][2] In Utah, you have 180 days to report but acting fast prevents disputes and starts benefits sooner.[1] Key factors: Document details of the incident; inform doctor it's work-related; get a copy of reports. Follow up if insurer doesn't decide within 21 days (or 45 with extension).[2] Practical guidance: Avoid signing anything from insurer without review; track all medical visits and expenses. Consult a workers' comp attorney if delayed. Workers' comp is no-fault, so focus on prompt reporting for medical and wage benefits.[1][2]
